element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Used throughout game as primary melee tool
Familiar Spell, Dragon Tooth Spell acquired through purchase/discovery; limited uses per spell
Used to purchase spells, weapons, armor upgrades, shields, helmets, keys, food at inns
Contains money, magic spells, or equipment upgrades; requires keys to open
Depletes from enemy damage, projectiles, or extended falls; replenished by chicken and brew pickups

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Second part of each domain (Windbane, Blightwater, Firesmite, Earthscorch)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Mandatory to damage corresponding Elemental bosses; domain-locked and consume magic resource
Must find and return to unlock second part of domain; gated progression mechanic

element_introduction_order.first_appears: End of second part of each domain
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Sequenced: Wind, then Earth, Fire, Water elementals; defeat yields IronSword pieces
Cumulative collection of 4 pieces; required for final boss confrontation
Water Spout Spell creates vertical traversal; Veil of Slumber slows enemies; Silver Fleece grants temporary invincibility
Book, cross, gauntlet, ring; high-value point rewards; enable Hall of Fame entry

element_introduction_order.element: Final boss battle (Malkil at IceFire Mountain)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Requires fully assembled IronSword; climax of campaign

typical_relief_triggers: Collecting golden bubbles for magic restoration
typical_tension_triggers: Mandatory elemental boss spells (must acquire to progress)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gated progression rhythm: explore domain → collect resources/currency → find artifact → unlock second part → acquire boss spell → defeat elemental → obtain IronSword piece. Cycle repeats 4 times with escalating challenge.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Water Spout Spell enables high-placed areas; treasure chests located throughout offer optional exploration rewards
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden relics (book, cross, gauntlet, ring) scattered in secret locations; proximity triggers gold bubble spawns indicating nearby secrets

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory: basic enemy encounters, sword combat foundation, currency/spell acquisition mechanics established, first elemental (Wind) presents manageable threat
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating: Earth and Fire elementals demand more complex spell acquisition sequences, greater resource management, tougher enemy patterns
difficulty_curve.late_game: Climactic: Water elemental presents heightened challenge, IceFire Mountain final confrontation with Malkil requires complete IronSword assembly and mastery of accumulated spells
notable_spikes: Elemental boss encounters (each requires specific spell and combat strategy)
Continue function loss bug after entering Earth domain (permadeath consequences)
Final Malkil battle (demands full IronSword and optimized spell/combat usage)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 4 domains splits into part 1 (artifact retrieval/exploration) and part 2 (boss spell acquisition/elemental confrontation). Artifact return gates access to part 2.
Bosses cannot be damaged without acquiring domain-specific elemental spell first. Spell must be found in second domain part. Creates mandatory gating and exploration incentive.
Money collected from enemies/treasures enables inn purchases of spells, equipment, keys. Equipment upgrades improve survivability; keys unlock treasure chests with additional resources.
Inns provide merchant functionality (buy food/keys/items), gambling opportunity (bonus chance), and implied safe zones for resource management.
Each elemental defeat yields one IronSword piece (4 total). Final boss requires all 4 pieces assembled. Creates escalating stakes across campaign.
